scores that Medicare uses to adjust payments to 
MA plans to account for differences in health status among plan enrollees. CMS expects MA plans with enrollees who have 
higher than average risk scores to incur higher than average costs; therefore, CMS adjusts the capitated payments to MA plans 
accordingly. Those risk scores are based in part on diagnosis codes that CMS then groups into related disease categories known 
as Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs).
